windows.transcript: wax to melt. This usually takes about five minutes.
windows.old_description: Olive/yellow wax in the pot — essential oil/fragrance added.
windows.embed_text_v2: Melting white wax flakes into clear liquid wax in a double boiler, inner black pot inside a larger pot on a wooden counter, flakes progressively liquefying
windows.summary_v2: Top-down time-lapse of white wax flakes melting down to clear liquid wax in a double-boiler pot setup.

tags_v2.action: melting wax flakes into liquid wax

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down view of a double boiler, an inner black pot set in a larger pot, holding a mound of white wax flakes half-melted into clear liquid wax.
The white wax flakes shrink as more melt into the growing pool of clear molten wax in the inner pot.
A crescent of unmelted white wax flakes remains at the rim while clear melted wax spreads across the pot.
Most of the wax has liquefied to translucent amber; a thinning patch of white flakes lingers.
The pot is mostly clear molten wax now, only a small island of pale flakes left melting.
Nearly all the wax is melted to clear liquid, faint specks of softening flakes floating.
The inner pot holds clear molten wax with a small smear of residual pale flakes near the centre.
Almost fully melted clear wax in the double boiler, just a few flecks of unmelted wax remaining.

windows.transcript: temperature is around 51 to 54 to 51 degrees. That's the exact temperature that is perfect for pouring and you won't have
windows.old_description: Yellow candle in the glass left to set.
windows.embed_text_v2: checking wax temperature with thermometer probe in black metal pouring pitcher of dark melted wax, overhead top-down view on wooden table, cooling before pouring candle
windows.summary_v2: An overhead shot holds on a black pouring pitcher of melted wax with a thermometer resting in it, checking that the wax has cooled to the correct pouring temperature.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead top-down view of a black melting pitcher on a wooden table holding dark melted wax, with a metal thermometer probe resting across the surface to check temperature before pouring.
The black pouring pot sits centered on the wood grain; the still pool of melted wax has a thermometer laid in it, its clip handle at the pitcher's left rim.
Same overhead shot of the wax-filled black pitcher; the surface is calm and dark as the thermometer monitors the cooling wax temperature.
Static top-down framing of the melted wax in the black metal pot with the thermometer probe, awaiting the right pour temperature.
The overhead view holds on the black pitcher of dark melted wax and resting thermometer on the wooden surface.
Unchanged overhead shot; the thermometer remains across the smooth dark wax pool in the black pouring pot.
Top-down view continues of the black melting pitcher with melted wax and thermometer, checking that the wax has cooled to pouring temperature.
Final overhead frame of the black pot holding dark melted wax with the thermometer probe, ready for pouring once at temperature.
